message:
feat(9zy): Implement self-update command with GitHub Releases integration

Add `rch update` command for self-updating binaries:
- Check for updates from GitHub Releases API
- Download and verify release artifacts with SHA256/BLAKE3 checksums
- Coordinate with daemon for safe updates (graceful shutdown)
- Support rollback to previous versions from timestamped backups
- Fleet update capability (stub) for updating all workers
- PID-based update lock to prevent concurrent updates

Also fixes incomplete Style→Theme refactoring in UI module by adding
type alias for backwards compatibility.

CLI options: --check, --version, --channel, --fleet, --rollback,
--verify, --yes, --dry-run, --no-restart, --drain-timeout, --show-changelog

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+

+
+async fn handle_update(
+    ctx: &OutputContext,
+    check_only: bool,
+    version: Option<String>,
+    channel: String,
+    fleet: bool,
+    do_rollback: bool,
+    verify_only: bool,
+    yes: bool,
+    dry_run: bool,
+    no_restart: bool,
+    drain_timeout: u64,
+    show_changelog: bool,
+) -> Result<()> {
+    let channel = channel
+        .parse::<update::Channel>()
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;
+
+    update::run_update(
+        ctx,
+        check_only,
+        version,
+        channel,
+        fleet,
+        do_rollback,
+        verify_only,
+        dry_run,
+        yes,
+        no_restart,
+        drain_timeout,
+        show_changelog,
+    )
+    .await
+    .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{}", e))
+}
